title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home. This easy recipe brings the taste of Korea to your kitchen. Get the best tips!
longTailKeywords: best Haemul Pajeon recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly made Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake, garnished with scallions and served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake perfect for any meal!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, packed with a delightful assortment of fresh seafood and vibrant scallions. It is a fantastic appetizer or side dish, perfect for sharing with friends and family, especially on a rainy day. This flavorful dish offers a wonderful textural contrast and a rich umami taste that will transport you straight to the bustling streets of Korea.

ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your assorted seafood, cutting larger pieces like squid into bite-sized rings or strips. Pat them dry thoroughly. Wash and trim the scallions, then cut them into roughly 3-inch long pieces. Thinly slice the onion and red chili pepper if using.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, salt, and cold water until a smooth batter forms. Make sure there are no lumps. Crack in the egg and whisk again until fully incorporated. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to a crepe batter.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, sliced onion, and red chili pepper (if using) to the batter. Gently fold everything together until the seafood and vegetables are evenly coated with the batter. Be careful not to overmix.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, toasted sesame seeds, and gochugaru (if using). Set aside. This sauce will perfectly complement the savory pancake.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the bottom of the pan evenly. Once the oil is hot, pour about half of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it out to form a thin, even layer. Arrange about half of the cut scallions neatly on top of the batter.

instructions.title: Achieve Golden Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Cook the pancake for 5-7 minutes on one side until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the bottom is cooked through. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges, then carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until it is also golden brown and crispy. Repeat with the remaining batter to make a second pancake.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy your crispy, savory Korean seafood pancake!

tips: For extra crispiness, use ice-cold water in your batter. This helps prevent gluten development and results in a lighter, crispier pancake.
Feel free to customize your seafood mix. Clams, oysters, or even crab meat can be added. Ensure all seafood is fresh and thoroughly cleaned.
Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed immediately after cooking when it's still hot and crispy. Pair it with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) for an authentic experience.
article:
Haemul Pajeon, the iconic Korean seafood and green onion pancake, is a culinary delight that has captivated taste buds worldwide. More than just a simple pancake, this savory dish is a symphony of textures and flavors – crispy edges yielding to a soft, chewy interior, studded with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Whether enjoyed as a comforting snack on a rainy day, a tantalizing appetizer at a bustling Korean restaurant, or a satisfying meal at home, Haemul Pajeon offers a delicious glimpse into the heart of Korean cuisine. Its popularity stems not only from its incredible taste but also its versatility and the communal joy it brings.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

At its core, Haemul Pajeon is a type of jeon, which refers to any dish made by pan-frying ingredients coated in a batter. The name itself offers a direct translation: "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pajeon" refers to a green onion pancake. So, it is literally a seafood and green onion pancake. What sets Haemul Pajeon apart is the generous inclusion of long strips of green onions, often laid out in an artful fashion, and a medley of fresh seafood embedded within a light, airy batter. The result is a substantial pancake, typically larger and thicker than other Korean jeon varieties, characterized by its golden-brown, crispy exterior and a tender, flavorful inside bursting with the briny taste of the sea and the pungent sweetness of green onions. It's often paired with makgeolli, a traditional Korean rice wine, especially during the rainy season, as the sizzling sound of the pancake is said to mimic the sound of falling rain.

## Ingredients for Korean Seafood Pancake

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon begins with selecting high-quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity and the freshness of its components.

For the **Batter**:
*   **Flour:** A combination of all-purpose flour and a small amount of rice flour or cornstarch is common. The rice flour/cornstarch is crucial for achieving that desirable crispy texture.
*   **Water or Broth:** Cold water or anchovy broth (for added umami) is used to create a light, lump-free batter.
*   **Egg:** One egg helps bind the batter and adds richness.
*   **Seasoning:** A pinch of salt and pepper.

For the **Seafood**:
*   A mix of fresh or frozen (and thawed) seafood is ideal. Common choices include:
    *   **Shrimp:** Peeled, deveined, and roughly chopped.
    *   **Squid:** Cleaned and cut into bite-sized rings or pieces.
    *   **Mussels or Clams:** Shelled.
    *   **Oysters:** Fresh or canned (drained).
    *   You can also add crab meat or baby octopus for extra flair.

For the **Vegetables**:
*   **Green Onions (Scallions):** These are the star! You'll need a large bunch, cut into 2-3 inch lengths.
*   **Other Vegetables (Optional):** Thinly sliced onion, red or green bell pepper strips, or even julienned carrots can add color and subtle flavor, though green onions should remain dominant.
*   **Garlic:** Minced garlic can be added to the batter or sautéed with the seafood for extra aroma.

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step

Making Haemul Pajeon is a rewarding experience, and with a few key steps, you can achieve restaurant-quality results at home.

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Green Onions:** Thoroughly clean and pat dry all your seafood. Cut shrimp and squid into manageable, bite-sized pieces. Cut the green onions into 2-3 inch lengths, separating the white and light green parts from the darker green tops.
2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (or cornstarch), salt, pepper, and egg. Gradually add the cold water or broth, whisking continuously until you have a smooth batter with the consistency of thin pancake batter – not too thick, not too thin. Avoid overmixing.
3.  **Combine Ingredients:** Gently fold the prepared seafood and most of the green onions (reserving some of the darker green tops for garnish during cooking) into the batter. Ensure everything is evenly coated.
4.  **Pan-Frying:** Heat a large, non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of cooking oil (about 2-3 tablespoons). Once the oil is hot, pour about 1.5 to 2 cups of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake about 1/2 to 3/4 inch thick. Immediately arrange the reserved green onion tops artistically on top of the batter.
5.  **Cook and Flip:** Cook for 4-6 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the batter appears mostly set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges. Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula (or two spatulas, or even a plate if it's very large). Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, until golden brown and cooked through. Press down gently with the spatula to ensure even crisping.
6.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board, slice it into wedges, and serve immediately while hot.

## Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that signature crispy exterior is key to a truly outstanding Haemul Pajeon. Here are some essential tips:

*   **Cold Water/Broth:** Using very cold liquid for your batter helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crispier pancake. Some even recommend using ice water.
*   **Rice Flour or Cornstarch:** Don't skip this! A small amount (about 1/4 to 1/3 ratio to all-purpose flour) will significantly enhance the crispiness.
*   **Generous Oil:** Think shallow-frying, not just sautéing. A good amount of hot oil is essential for developing that golden, crunchy crust.
*   **Hot Pan, Then Medium Heat:** Start with a hot pan to get an initial sear, then reduce to medium to ensure the inside cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Give your pancake space. If making multiple, use a large enough pan or cook in batches.
*   **Thin Batter:** A slightly thinner batter spreads better and cooks up crispier. If your batter is too thick, add a little more cold water.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from the seafood can make the pancake soggy. Ensure all seafood is thoroughly patted dry before adding to the batter.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed hot, straight from the pan, cut into wedges. It's a fantastic standalone snack or appetizer, but it also pairs wonderfully with a variety of Korean side dishes (banchan) and beverages.

The traditional pairing for Haemul Pajeon, especially on a rainy day, is a bowl of **makgeolli** (Korean rice wine). Its slightly sweet and milky effervescence complements the savory, rich flavors of the pancake perfectly.

A simple yet flavorful **dipping sauce** is essential. Here's a classic recipe:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional, for a kick)
*   1 clove minced garlic (optional)
*   1 tablespoon finely chopped green onions (optional)
Combine all ingredients and mix well. Adjust to your taste preferences, aiming for a balance of salty, tangy, and a hint of nutty sesame.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a masterpiece, its versatility allows for exciting variations:

*   **Seafood Choices:** Feel free to customize the seafood to your liking or what's available. Clams, baby octopus, or even surimi (crab sticks) can be used.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Beyond green onions, you can incorporate other vegetables like finely shredded carrots, zucchini, or even a touch of kimchi for a spicy, tangy twist (though then it leans towards kimchi jeon).
*   **Meat Options:** While not strictly "Haemul Pajeon," you can make similar savory pancakes with thinly sliced beef (yukjeon) or pork (gogi jeon) for a different flavor profile.
*   **Gluten-Free:** For a gluten-free option, use only rice flour or a gluten-free flour blend for the batter. The texture might be slightly different but still delicious.
*   **Vegan:** Omit the egg from the batter and use an egg substitute or simply more water/broth. Focus on a rich medley of vegetables and perhaps some plant-based "seafood" alternatives if desired.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its comforting warmth, satisfying crunch, and rich flavors make it a beloved staple in Korean cuisine. Don't hesitate to try making this delightful Korean seafood pancake at home – it’s a culinary adventure that promises to tantalize your taste buds and bring a piece of Korea to your kitchen.
